B: The signpost is terrifyingly huge!
C: The directional sign is huge!

standard: Lâu rồi không thấy ông bán kem chanh đấy.
A: It's been a long time since I last saw that guy selling lemon ice cream.
B: I haven't seen that lemon ice cream seller for a long time.
C: It's been a long time since I've seen that lemon ice cream seller.

standard: Vậy Quảng Bình về Quảng Bình làm rể được không?
A: So, can someone from Quang Binh marry into another province and become a son-in-law there?
B: So, can a person from Quang Binh become a son-in-law in Quang Binh?
C: So can someone from Quảng Bình go back to Quảng Bình and become a son-in-law there?

standard: Thương cái gì. 1 mùa hè năm nay là no nứt rốn.
A: What's there to love? This summer I've had so much to eat my fill.
B: What is there to pity? One summer this year and I'll be stuffed to the gills.
C: About what. This year's summer is so full you could burst.

standard: Các cháu càng ngày càng giỏi nhỉ?
A: The kids are getting better and better, aren't they?
B: The children are getting better and better, aren't they?
C: You kids are getting better and better, huh?

standard: ăn đứt mấy đội rởm này.
A: They're far better than those lousy teams.
B: Far better than these fake teams.
C: We can easily beat these fake teams.

standard: Do gu hay sao chứ mình không thấy đẹp.
A: It's either a matter of taste or something, because I don't find it beautiful.
B: Maybe it's just my taste, but I don't find it beautiful.
C: Maybe it's just a matter of taste, but I don't find it beautiful.

standard: Công minh đâu chứ đâu phải công minh này.
A: This is definitely not the kind of fairness that exists here.
B: Fairness is one thing, but this isn't that kind of fairness.
C: Where is the fairness? This isn't fairness at all.

standard: về có bầu cua tôm cá gà heo chứ.
A: It's about having crab, shrimp, fish, chicken, and pork.
B: Are you bringing the Gourd Crab Shrimp Fish Chicken Pig game back?
C: I brought back gourd, crab, shrimp, fish, rooster, and pig, that's for sure.

standard: nhà ngoại em bên cạnh nhà đấy.
A: My maternal grandparents' house is next door.
B: My maternal grandparents' house is next to that house.
C: My maternal grandparents' house is next to that house.

standard: Không làm gì thì không phải ăn đâu.
A: If you don't do anything, you don't have to eat.
B: If you don't do anything, you don't get to eat.
C: No work, no food.

standard: Chắc muốn ở Mỹ ấy mà! Việt Nam ta không có làm được thế này đâu.
A: They probably want to live in America! We in Vietnam can't do this.
B: They probably want to be in the US! In our Vietnam, this can't be done.
C: Must want to be in America! Our Vietnam can't do this.

standard: Thấy vậy chứ sửa toàn bộ cả rồi.
A: It may look like that, but everything has been completely fixed.
B: It may seem that way, but everything has already been fixed.
C: It might look that way, but I've already fixed it all.

standard: Chắc cũng chưa anh ạ. Em chưa thấy đeo túi đó.
A: Probably not, sir. I haven't seen him carrying that bag.
B: Probably not yet. I haven't seen them wearing that bag.
C: Probably not yet, anh. I haven't seen anyone wear that bag.

standard: Chưa giỏi đâu mà nhường họ thế.
A: You're not that good yet, so don't give way to them.
B: I'm not even that good yet, why give in to them like that?
C: Not even good at it yet, yet I let them go like that.

standard: Vậy nhà của dượng nhà mày à.
A: So, is that your uncle's house?
B: So that's your uncle's house.
C: So that's your uncle's house, huh?

standard: Không biết trái gì!
A: I don't know what kind of fruit it is!
B: I don't know what fruit this is!
C: I don't know what's wrong.

standard: thật chứ kiểu như vậy chắc không dám lấy chồng luôn đấy.
A: Honestly, someone like that probably wouldn't dare get married.
B: Seriously, if it's like that, I probably wouldn't dare get married.
C: Seriously, if it's like this, I probably wouldn't dare get married at all.

standard: sao hay vậy Phương nhỉ, sao lại vứt quà bánh thế kia
A: That's strange, Phuong, why did you throw away the gifts and snacks like that?
B: How is that possible, Phuong? Why throw away the gifts and sweets like that?
C: How come, Phương? Why did you throw away the gifts like that?

standard: Yên Bái nhiều người đẹp nhỉ
A: Yen Bai has so many beautiful women, doesn't it?
B: Yen Bai has many beautiful people, doesn't it?
C: Yên Bái has a lot of beautiful people, right?

standard: nghe giải thưởng thấy ngon vậy nhỉ.
A: That sounds like a great prize!
B: The prize sounds so tempting, doesn't it?
C: Hearing about the prize, it sounds really good, doesn't it?

standard: bão luôn chứ mưa là gì?
A: It's always a storm, not just rain!
B: It's a storm, not just rain.
C: It's a storm, not just rain!

standard: Nghèo khổ thế này là do con cái sa đọa với mai thúy
A: This poverty is due to the children's depravity and drug addiction.
B: Being this poor is because the children are degenerate and use drugs.
C: We're so poor because the kids have gone astray with Mai Thúy.

standard: Ông ở nhà làm nồi lẩu. Thích hơn nhỉ?
A: He'd rather stay home and make hot pot. Isn't that better?
B: I'll stay home and make a hotpot. That's better, right?
C: I'm home making a pot of hot pot. It's better this way, isn't it?

standard: Nhìn thấy FE CREDIT thì có xinh mấy thấy cũng ngán ngẩm.
A: Seeing FE CREDIT makes even the prettiest girl feel disgusted.
B: Seeing FE CREDIT, no matter how beautiful they are, it's still discouraging.
C: Seeing FE CREDIT, no matter how appealing it looks, I still feel fed up.

standard: Mở mắt ra là thấy vàng liền luôn trời, ngày nào cũng lên cao khiếp thế nhỉ?
A: The first thing I see when I open my eyes is gold! It keeps rising so high every day, doesn't it?
B: I open my eyes and see gold immediately, why does it go up so terrifyingly every day?
C: Just opened my eyes and gold prices already skyrocketed, going up every single day - that's really something!

standard: tìm một chỗ đứng xem cho đã.
A: Find a good spot to stand and watch.
B: Find a spot to stand and watch to my heart's content.
C: Find a good spot to watch first.

standard: Thèm thì về làm thịt cho mà ăn chị ơi, gà này họ làm xúc xích hết rồi.
A: If you're craving it, come home and I'll butcher it for you, sister. They've already made sausages from all this chicken.
B: If you're craving it, come home and I'll slaughter some for you to eat, sister; these chickens have all been made into sausages.
C: If you're craving it, come home and slaughter one for yourself to eat, sister. They've made all the chickens into sausages already.

standard: Sáng qua tôi mới chọc mấy chị môi trường. Vật xuống làm gì để giờ phải chổng mông đở lên.
A: Yesterday morning I teased the environmental ladies. Why did you throw it down only to have to lift it up with your butt sticking up in the air?
B: Yesterday morning, I just teased the sanitation workers. Why did I mess with them, and now I have to scramble to make things right.
C: Yesterday morning I just poked at some bees, and now I have to stick my butt up to catch them as they fall.

standard: tận 2 chỉ nhé không phải 1, trúng mãi luôn kìa.
A: That's two taels, not just one! They keep winning!
B: It's a full 2 mace, not 1; I keep winning.
C: As many as 2, not just 1, won on both repeatedly!

standard: Ai mà ơn trời vậy nhỉ?
A: Who is so lucky to have received such a blessing from heaven?
B: Who is the one that is a godsend?
C: Who on earth is that blessed soul?

standard: lớn rồi có con rồi không được hỗn nhé
A: You're grown up now, you have children, so don't be disrespectful.
B: You're grown up and have children now, so don't be disrespectful.
C: You're all grown up and have kids now, don't be naughty anymore, okay?

standard: đường ra ruộng nước sâu lút đầu gối.
A: The path to the rice fields was flooded up to our knees.
B: The road to the field has water deep up to the knees.
C: The path to the field is filled with knee-deep water.

standard: Hồi đó ngây thơ, bây giờ là con rắn độc
A: She was innocent back then, but now she's a venomous snake.
B: I was naive back then, now I'm a poisonous snake.
C: Back then I was naive, now I'm a venomous snake

standard: tôi ngủ 2 giấc dậy còn thấy bà nhậu mà.
A: I slept for two naps and when I woke up I still saw her drinking.
B: I woke up from two naps and still saw her drinking.
C: I took two naps and still saw her drinking, though.

standard: không có tiền thì làm sao để có nhỉ?
A: How can you get something if you don't have money?
B: If I don't have any money, how can I get some?
C: How can I get some when I don't have any money?

standard: Đâu dám có người yêu chứ.
A: I wouldn't dare have a boyfriend.
B: I wouldn't dare have a lover.
C: I'm way too single for that.

standard: Chắc đòi mua SH mà không chịu nên đi đó.
A: He probably wanted to buy an SH scooter but they refused, so he left.
B: They probably went there because they wanted to buy an SH but were refused.
C: Probably demanding to buy an SH [scooter] but refusing to work for it, so [they] went there.

standard: Ở đoạn nào mà ngập dữ vậy?
A: Which area is experiencing such severe flooding?
B: Which section is so badly flooded?
C: Which part is flooded that badly?

standard: Bố này đáng yêu nhỉ. Lo cho con thế này thì chắc thương con lắm.
A: This dad is so sweet! He must love his child very much, showing so much concern for him.
B: This dad is so lovely. Caring for his child like this, he must love his child very much.
C: This dad is so sweet. Worrying about his child like this, he must love the child very much.

standard: Ăn ngon lắm nhé, ăn mãi rồi nghiện luôn
A: It tastes so good, I've gotten addicted to it!
B: It tastes delicious; I've been eating it so much that I'm addicted.
C: It's so delicious, I've been eating it so much I've become hooked.

standard: Chứ định đi ăn đồ nướng à?
A: Are you planning to go eat barbecue instead?
B: So you're planning to go eat grilled food?
C: So you're planning to go eat barbecue?

standard: Về đi chứ! Bên đó loạn rồi.
A: Come back! It's chaos over there.
B: Come back already! It's chaotic over there.
C: Come back already! It's chaos over there.

standard: Thôi chị đừng mơ nữa. Vừa rồi chị mơ là nhà em ngập đấy. Em liệt đi tê.
A: Stop dreaming, sister. You just dreamt that my house was flooded. I was paralyzed.
B: Stop dreaming. You just dreamed that my house was flooded. I'm totally paralyzed.
C: Stop dreaming, don't expect that anymore. You were just wishing my house would get flooded. Are you out of your mind?

standard: nhìn là thèm chỉ muốn về chợ Mỹ Chánh ngay.
A: Just looking at it makes me crave it and want to go to My Chanh market right away.
B: It looks so delicious that I just want to go back to My Chanh market right now.
C: Just looking at it makes me crave it, I just want to go to My Chanh market right away.

standard: Chắc phải về làm cái nhỉ!
A: I guess I'll have to go home and do it!
B: I should probably go back and make one!
C: I guess I should go back and do it.

standard: Xe tăng với thiết giáp đi cơ mà
A: Tanks and armored vehicles are going there.
B: But tanks and armored vehicles were going.
C: Tanks and armored vehicles go by what though

standard: Team này đoạt giải bạn nhỉ?
A: This team won the prize, right?
B: This team won the prize, didn't they?
C: This team won the prize, right?

standard: Bà Na chứ bé nào.
A: It's Mrs. Na, not a child.
B: It's Mrs. Na, not some little girl.
C: It's Bà Na, not some kid.

standard: trời ơi sao mà tội vậy nhỉ, nhìn mà khóc ấy, đoạn này góc khuất quá hả em?
A: Oh my God, how pitiful! It makes me want to cry. Is this scene too hidden from view?
B: Oh my god, why is it so pitiful? Looking at this makes me want to cry. Is this part in too much of a blind spot?
C: oh my god, why is it so pitiful, looking at it just makes you want to cry, this part is too dark and hidden, right?

standard: Muốn làm rể Kiên Giang để thử tửu lượng một phát xem nào
A: I want to become a son-in-law in Kien Giang to test my drinking capacity.
B: I want to become a son-in-law of Kien Giang to test my alcohol tolerance once.
C: Want to become a son-in-law in Kiên Giang just to test my alcohol tolerance once and see

standard: à mà chưa có tấm nào trong này hết
A: Oh, but there aren't any pictures in here yet.
B: Oh, by the way, there aren't any photos in here yet.
C: Ah, but I don't have any photos in here at all

standard: Có Khi nào bà ấy với ông ấy thành đôi không nhỉ
A: I wonder if she and he will ever become a couple?
B: I wonder if she and he will ever become a couple.
C: I wonder if she and him will ever become a couple.

standard: khi nào lên trên này nhậu
A: When are you coming up here for a drink?
B: When are you coming up here to drink?
C: When are you coming up here to drink?

standard: Ở chỗ nào chúng mày biết không, chúng mày?
A: Do you guys know where it is?
B: Do you guys know where it is, you guys?
C: Do you guys even know where it is?
